length2_dominant_length2_rare_noise_5_to.description: Both dominant and rare bodies have length two; rare body is noise.

length2_dominant_singleton_rare_noise_4_.description: Dominant length-2 body with singleton rare body as noise.

rare_count_two_noise_5_to_2.description: Rare body count two remains outside the singleton-rare filter.

singleton_dominant_length2_rare_valid_4_.description: Dominant singleton body with length-2 rare body as valid behavior.

singleton_noise_2_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 2; singleton rare body is treated as noise.

singleton_noise_3_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 3; singleton rare body is treated as noise.

singleton_noise_4_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 4; singleton rare body is treated as noise.

singleton_noise_5_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 5; singleton rare body is treated as noise.

singleton_valid_rare_2_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 2; singleton rare body is valid held-out behavior.

singleton_valid_rare_3_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 3; singleton rare body is valid held-out behavior.

singleton_valid_rare_4_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 4; singleton rare body is valid held-out behavior.

singleton_valid_rare_5_to_1.description: Dominant singleton body support 5; singleton rare body is valid held-out behavior.

two_rare_bodies_noise_4_1_1.description: Two singleton rare bodies are noise; weaker dominant share may keep them.

two_rare_bodies_one_valid_5_1_1.description: One singleton rare body is valid and one is noise; support alone cannot distinguish them.

validation_no_body_signal_unresolved.description: Validation probes do not distinguish keeping versus filtering the rare body.

validation_positive_negative_conflict.description: The same rare-body trace appears as both validation positive and negative.

validation_selects_filter_rare_3_to_1.description: Validation negatives mark unseen rare-body combinations invalid, so the selector should filter the rare body.

validation_selects_keep_rare_3_to_1.description: Validation positives require the rare body, so the selector should keep all bodies.

validation_training_conflict.description: Validation negatives contain an observed rare training trace, so body exclusion conflicts with training replay.
